title: plagiobothrys salsus
intro: Plagiobothrys salsus, also known as salt marsh blisters, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. This plant is primarily found in coastal areas and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Plagiobothrys salsus are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tinctures, salves, and infusions, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne.
